B1.best-aspect: The text wasn't too hard to understand.
B1.tlx-effort-reason: Understanding the explanation wasn't the hard part: checking whether the explanation's answer is actually correct was.
B1.tlx-mental-reason:
As I stated above, understanding the text wasn't too hard.
However, it had some cognitive load compared to other systems, as there were no animations and only text for this system.
B1.tlx-temporal-reason: Because I had to go through the tedious step of calculating everything by hand, I felt a bit rushed during the calculation because it took so much of my time.
B1.transparency-reason: A large reason on why this system wasn't useful for me is because it didn't include transparency for each of its calculations.
B1.trust-reason: Most explanations were pretty straightforward, but the explanation didn't include specific steps for calculation.
B1.usefulness-reason:
Although the explanations themselves were 'correct', it didn't help answer the chart QA for two reasons:
1. The explanation didn't provide specific calculation processes, so I had to go through each calculation by hand to check whether the system's calculation was correct.
2. Another reason why I had to check the calculations was because none of the final answers matched the explanation's answers. So, I had to find out which of the two answer was correct by hand.
B1.worst-aspect: Please align the final answer and the explanation's answer: this was extremely frustrating for the user (who had to check whether the answer was right or wrong).

B2.best-aspect: The markers helped me understand the reasoning.
B2.tlx-effort-reason: Understanding the explanation was relatively easy.
B2.tlx-mental-reason: The explanations themselves were straightforward, especially with markers (although some parts of the reasoning were wrong).
B2.tlx-performance-reason: Understanding the explanation was relatively easy thanks to the markers for each reasoning step.
B2.tlx-temporal-reason: I felt no feeling of being rushed because the reasoning were presented step-by-step.
B2.transparency-reason: The markers made me understand what the system was actually thinking for each reasoning step, so it was easy to pinpoint errors in reasoning.
B2.trust-reason: This system produced too much errors within the reasoning step. Also, the animations tied to some reasoning steps were completely wrong. This made me not trust the system in general.
B2.usefulness-reason:
The explanations were sometimes correct, but I noticed that this system produces lots of 'labels' and 'arrows' on the chart for each reasoning step. Some of the added markers in the visualization wasn't aligned properly with actual values, making me frustrated and hindering my understanding of the chart.
Also, some explanations produced hallucinations (for example, choosing a random value when the reasoning indicated it found the largest value), so the explanations were more of a burden than of actual use.
B2.worst-aspect: The markers were sometimes not drawn accurately (in their correct position), and there were multiple situations of hallucinations.

B3.best-aspect: Animations were cool, especially transforming chart types.
B3.tlx-effort-reason: Same with the prevous answer; understanding transformed charts came as a cognitive burden.
B3.tlx-mental-reason: This system frequently uses the method of transforming chart types for better explanability (such as transforming stacked bar charts into grouped bar charts). This did enhance understanding eventually, but at first glance it was hard to determine why the chart suddenly changed so much. Users not familiar to data visualization may have a harder time understanding the system.
B3.tlx-performance-reason:
For the 'average of maximum payment in all states' question, changing the x values for grouped bar charts suddenly made me look through the chart multiple times to understand it clearly.
(Maybe I would have understood it better if the transformed charts had different color schemes.)
Other than this occasion, I was successful in understanding all other explanations.
B3.tlx-temporal-reason: I just had to follow the step-by-step reasoning.
B3.transparency-reason: The explanations were transparent, as the system added markers and numbers on top of the chart that helps understand the reasoning process.
B3.trust-reason:
This system also included multiple occasions of hallucinations. For example, the system sometimes didn't follow the question's requirements (measuring values between 20 and 30 when the chart question asked for values between 19 and 30) or failed to understand the values in the chart (reasoning that two values were at a near-tie when one is clearly bigger than the other). 
Also, the final answer didn't match the answer from the reasoning, which made me trust the system even less.
B3.usefulness-reason:
The explanations were useful, but some had accuracy issues. Animations also helped me understand the explanations.
This system transformed chart types for better explanability (such as transforming stacked bar charts into grouped bar charts), which did make the explanation more useful.
B3.worst-aspect: This system still had too many hallucinations and incorrect final answers, so the actual accuracy need to be improved.

Ours.best-aspect: The animation paired with each reasoning. I loved how the animation highlighted (in 'bold') the relevant parts of the chart QA.
Ours.tlx-effort-reason: The animations lowered the cognitive load on understanding the explanation (though I had to double-check to see if the reasoning & animation were actually correct).
Ours.tlx-mental-reason: The animations made the explanation very easy to understand.
Ours.tlx-temporal-reason: The step-by-step reasoning made sense, so I could just follow the reasoning steps without thinking too much outside of the reasoning or rushing to get the final answer.
Ours.transparency-reason: I remember there was one wrong answer for this system (England & Wales part), but the reasoning step was transparent enough that I could pinpoint where and how the reasoning went wrong. So, although the reasoning was wrong and the final answer was also wrong, I could see how the system reasoned and came up with the final answer.
Ours.trust-reason: All but 1 explanation was correct, and the visualization was immaculate.
Ours.usefulness-reason: The reasoning and the animation paired with each reasoning step made me understand the data visualization better.
Ours.worst-aspect: Some problems with reasoning (one question asked to pinpoint the 'smallest value', but the system pinpointed the 'second-smallest value' in its reasoning step), but the system was transparent enough that a human could find the errorneous step without too much cognitive load.
